scenes.text: In this scene, we'll explore the fundamental concept of JavaScript functions. Functions are one of the core building blocks of JavaScript programming. They let you wrap code in a package that you can easily reuse. Understanding functions is critical to writing clean and efficient code as they help in breaking down complex problems into smaller, manageable pieces.

In this scene, we'll dive into JavaScript's ability to handle asynchronous operations using promises. Asynchronous programming is key when performing long-running tasks such as data fetching from an API. Promises make it easier to write cleaner code that executes asynchronously by preventing callback hell, improving readability and maintainability.
scenes.image_prompt:
# Promises in JavaScript

## Asynchronous Operation

```javascript
// Fetching data with a promise
fetch('https://api.example.com/data')
    .then(response => response.json())
    .then(data => console.log(data))
    .catch(error => console.error('Error:', error));
```

### Execution Flow:

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant JS
    participant Server
    JS->>Server: Fetch data request
    Server-->>JS: Response with data
    JS->>JS: Process data
```
